header.description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets

oldVersionNotice.displayName: New node version available: get the latest version with added features from the nodes panel.

upsert.description: Create a new record, or update the current one if it already exists (upsert)

delete.description: Delete columns and rows from a sheet

lookup.description: Look up a specific column value and return the matching row

sheetId.description: The ID of the Google Spreadsheet. Found as part of the sheet URL https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{ID}/.

range.description: The table range to read from or to append data to. See the Google documentation for the details. If it contains multiple sheets it can also be added like this: "MySheet!A:F"

toDelete.description: Deletes columns and rows from a sheet

sheetId.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

startIndex.description: The start index (0 based and inclusive) of column to delete

sheetId.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

startIndex.description: The start index (0 based and inclusive) of row to delete

sheetId.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

startIndex.description: The start index (0 based and inclusive) of column to delete

sheetId.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

startIndex.description: The start index (0 based and inclusive) of row to delete

rawData.description: Whether the data supplied is RAW instead of parsed into keys

dataProperty.description: The name of the property from which to read the RAW data

dataStartRow.description: Index of the first row which contains the actual data and not the keys. Starts with 0.

keyRow.description: Index of the row which contains the keys. Starts at 0. The incoming node data is matched to the keys for assignment. The matching is case sensitive.

lookupColumn.description: The name of the column in which to look for value

lookupValue.description: The value to look for in column

key.description: The name of the key to identify which data should be updated in the sheet

continue.description: By default, the workflow stops executing if the lookup/read does not return values

returnAllMatches.description: By default only the first result gets returned. If options gets set all found matches get returned.

usePathForKeyRow.displayName: Use Header Names as JSON Paths
usePathForKeyRow.description: Whether you want to match the headers as path, for example, the row header "category.name" will match the "category" object and get the field "name" from it. By default "category.name" will match with the field with exact name, not nested object.

valueInputMode.description: Determines how data should be interpreted

RAW.description: The values will not be parsed and will be stored as-is

USER_ENTERED.description: The values will be parsed as if the user typed them into the UI. Numbers will stay as numbers, but strings may be converted to numbers, dates, etc. following the same rules that are applied when entering text into a cell via the Google Sheets UI.

valueRenderMode.description: Determines how values should be rendered in the output

FORMATTED_VALUE.description: Values will be calculated & formatted in the reply according to the cell's formatting.Formatting is based on the spreadsheet's locale, not the requesting user's locale. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return "$1.23".

FORMULA.description: Values will not be calculated. The reply will include the formulas. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return "=A1".

UNFORMATTED_VALUE.description: Values will be calculated, but not formatted in the reply. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return the number 1.23.

locale.description:
The locale of the spreadsheet in one of the following formats:
  • en (639-1)
  • fil (639-2 if no 639-1 format exists)
  • en_US (combination of ISO language an country)

ON_CHANGE.description: Volatile functions are updated on every change

MINUTE.description: Volatile functions are updated on every change and every minute

HOUR.description: Volatile functions are updated on every change and hourly

columnCount.description: The number of columns in the grid

columnGroupControlAfter.description: Whether the column grouping control toggle is shown after the group

frozenColumnCount.description: The number of columns that are frozen in the grid

frozenRowCount.description: The number of rows that are frozen in the grid

hideGridlines.description: Whether the grid isn't showing gridlines in the UI

rowCount.description: The number of rows in the grid

rowGroupControlAfter.description: Whether the row grouping control toggle is shown after the group

hidden.description: Whether the sheet is hidden in the UI, false if it's visible

rightToLeft.description: Whether the sheet is an RTL sheet instead of an LTR sheet

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet. Must be non-negative. This field cannot be changed once set.

index.description: The index of the sheet within the spreadsheet

tabColor.description: The color of the tab in the UI

hidden.description: Whether the Sheet should be hidden in the UI

title.description: Title of the property to create

hidden.description: Whether the Sheet should be hidden in the UI

title.description: Title of the property to create

simple.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

id.description: The ID of the sheet to delete
